paper.abstract_en: The present work aimed at the synthesis of the hitherto unknown <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Am</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>231</mn></mmultiscripts></math> and known <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Am</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>229</mn></mmultiscripts></math> isotopes via the <math><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>n</mi></mrow></math> evaporation channels of the fusion reactions of <math><mrow><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>48</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>+</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>Re</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>187</mn></mmultiscripts></mrow></math> and <math><mrow><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>48</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>+</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>Re</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>185</mn></mmultiscripts></mrow></math>, respectively. The new isotope <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Am</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>231</mn></mmultiscripts></math>, with a half-life of <math><mrow><msubsup><mn>47</mn><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>19</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>85</mn></mrow></msubsup><mspace width="0.28em"/><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></math>, was identified on the basis of five observed <math><mi>α</mi></math>-decay chains. It emits <math><mi>α</mi></math> particles with an energy of 7.11(2) MeV. An upper limit of 0.66 was estimated for the electron-capture decay branch of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Am</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>231</mn></mmultiscripts></math>. No <math><mi>α</mi></math>-decay chain from <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Am</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>229</mn></mmultiscripts></math> was observed, corresponding to a cross-section level of 0.03 nb for the detection of a single event. The <math><mi>α</mi></math>-decay properties of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Am</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>231</mn></mmultiscripts></math> are discussed in the framework of the Geiger-Nuttall empirical relation.
